LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE REQUEST ASKS THE COURT TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE "FOLLOWING FACTS" BUT THEN DOES NOT IDENTIFY THE SPECIFIC, INSTEAD ATTACHING THREE STATE/ASSEMBLY BILLS. EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO GRANT THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, THE CITED DOCUMENTS DO NOT CONCLUSIVELY DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT IS NOT LIABLE FOR THE INCIDENT IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT RAISES FACTUAL ISSUES THAT A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION OR TRIAL SHOULD RESOLVE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. Off Calendar Sep-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. The court denies the request for judicial notice. The request asks the Court to take judicial notice of the "following facts" but then does not identify the specific, instead attaching three state/assembly bills. Even if the court were to grant the request for judicial notice, the cited documents do not conclusively demonstrate that defendant is not liable for the incident in the Second Amended Complaint. Defendant raises factual issues that a summary judgment motion or trial should resolve. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Susan Kawala, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5708, for defendant and moving party;
Brittany Rogers, Esq. of the Law Office of Scott Righthand, Tel. 415.544.0115, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).) As to the first cause of action for negligence and the second cause of action for dangerous condition of public property, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to state a statutory basis for relief against Defendant State of California ("Defendant"). (See Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 795 ("statutory causes of action must be pleaded with particularity if applicable").) The third and fourth grounds for demurrer (as stated in Defendant's Notice of and Demurrer to First Amended Complaint) are overruled. For these grounds, Defendant relies on a request for judicial notice of extrinsic evidence to show that Defendant Kate Cuevas is not a state employee and that Defendant does not own or control the property at issue. The request for judicial notice of the truth of the matters asserted in documents on websites is denied. (Searles Valley Minerals Operations, Inc. v. State Bd. of Equalization (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 514, 519 (affirming denial of request for judicial notice of materials contained on the website pages of the American Coal Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy).) The third and fourth grounds for demurrer thus raise questions of fact that are not the proper basis for a demurrer. The fifth ground for demurrer, pertaining to the Second Cause of Action for Dangerous Condition, is overruled. Plaintiff alleges a dangerous condition on both the railroad tracks and the adjacent property. (Gov. Code section 830(a); FAC paras. 19-21.). The sixth ground for demurrer (failure to identify any physical defect) is overruled because Defendant does not make an argument in support of that ground in its moving papers. To the extent that it is part of the fifth ground for demurrer, it is overruled for the reasons stated above. The seventh ground for demurrer is overruled. The allegations of the FAC do not appear to implicate Government Code section 818.2 and 845; in any event, a demurrer will not lie to only part of a cause of action. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
